title: Multi-horizon pit specialist
summary: Next-lap pit labels are noisy at the edge of a window, while survival-style models learn the event-time shape directly.
rationale: Next-lap pit labels are noisy at the edge of a window, while survival-style models learn the event-time shape directly. Public event-time work suggests modeling several horizons can outperform a single binary cutoff.
implementation_hint: Train a specialist for `Pit within 1/2/3/5 laps` or a discrete hazard/PMF model, generate strict OOF predictions, and add derived features such as `P<=1`, `P<=3`, `P<=5`, `P<=1 / P<=3`, and horizon spread or entropy to the final ensemble.
expected_effect: Sharper discrimination between cars whose window is merely opening and cars likely to box immediately.
risk: Close to earlier time-to-pit ideas, so lift may be incremental unless the multi-horizon outputs are used as features rather than a standalone replacement.
